raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 309 aims to establish social housing in California as a universal human right.",
    "AB 309 has made it the furthest any social housing bill has moved in the California State Legislature.",
    "The proposed Social Housing Program under AB 309 will develop up to three social housing projects on state-owned excess land.",
    "The Social Housing Program aims to rent units at no more than 30 percent of residents' income.",
    "A home ownership model will be employed within the Social Housing Program.",
    "Access to affordable homes is out of reach for many Californians, as indicated by a 2023 survey by the Public Policy Institute of California.",
    "About one third of Californians considered moving out of state in 2023, up from 15% in 2004.",
    "Social housing can help tackle the housing crisis by producing homes protected from the for-profit market.",
    "Social housing accommodates residents of mixed incomes and is designed to be built sustainably and beautifully.",
    "Social housing aims to complement existing efforts to address California's housing crisis without over-reliance on government subsidies."
  ]
}
```
